home.hero_p1: At the Center for European Health Policies, we work to improve healthcare access and outcomes by partnering with organizations to develop and implement innovative strategies.
home.hero_p2: Our team is composed of experienced professionals with diverse backgrounds and expertise.
home.pillar1_li3: Design and build companies for sustainable growth
home.pillar2_title: Medical devices & IVDS regulation, marketing & distribution support
home.welcome_name: Prof. Dr. Albena Zlatareva, MD, PhD
home.welcome_quote: "The European healthcare market can be considered a component of national security, particularly in middle-income countries. An aging population, a severe demographic crisis, a higher prevalence of chronic diseases, and rising healthcare costs define it. At the same time, there is a strong push toward a patient-centric system driven by rapid advancements in medical technology and an increasingly restrictive regulatory environment. To navigate these challenges effectively, high-quality expertise and services that integrate these critical aspects will support strategic decision-making and successful market implementation."

team.hero_p: Center for European Health Policies (CEHP) is a consulting company focused on medical devices, IVDs, health technology assessment, and Medtech innovation across Europe.
team.mission_p: To empower healthcare organizations and MedTech companies with expert consulting in medical devices regulation, clinical performance evaluation, and health technology assessment, ensuring safe and effective products reach European markets.
team.focus_p: We specialize in MDR/IVDR compliance, CE marking, clinical trials for medical devices, conformity assessment, technical dossier preparation, secondary manufacturing, and market access strategies for MedDevs and IVDs in the European Union.
team.vision_p: To be the leading European consulting partner for health technology assessment and medical device regulatory affairs, driving innovation and patient access through data-driven insights and deep industry expertise in the Medtech ecosystem.
team.team_subtitle: Our multidisciplinary team combines academic excellence with deep industry experience to deliver comprehensive solutions across the full MedTech value chain.
team.team_card1_p: Expert regulatory consultants providing strategic guidance on MDR/IVDR compliance, CE marking, clinical evaluation, and market access for medical devices and IVDs across European markets.
team.team_card2_title: Strategy Development and Project Creating Specialists
team.team_card2_p: Dedicated professionals designing tailored strategies for medical device companies, from regulatory pathway planning and HTA submissions to clinical development programs and manufacturing optimization.
team.team_card3_title: Regulatory, Clinical and Market Access Teams
team.team_card3_p: Specialized teams covering the complete regulatory lifecycle: from clinical investigation design and technical dossier preparation to conformity assessment and post-market surveillance, ensuring seamless market access.
team.best_li1: Empowering our customers by accessing services, solutions and health data to realize their business goals.
team.best_li2: Providing a consistent, reliable, and effective platform for using health data for research, innovation, supporting policy and strategy development, achieving business objectives, regulatory issues, market access and addressing unmet patient needs.
team.best_biz_li1: Support Market access, sales, market share and existing expansion.
team.best_biz_li3: Business development: help to attract new business partners and investors.
team.best_hc_li1: Support positioning of the client as a trusted partner of all stakeholders in the healthcare system - institutions, healthcare providers, patients.
team.best_hc_li2: Support the client to be an Equal Partner in value-based decision-making in a patient-centered system.
team.best_hc_li4: Help to Improve the local health environment and patient health outcomes.
team.experts_subtitle: Our team combines academic excellence with deep industry experience in medical device regulation, clinical trials, health technology assessment, and Medtech innovation.
team.expert1_bio: MD, PhD. Expert in medical devices regulation, health technology assessment (HTA), and European health policy. Leads CEHP's strategic vision and consulting practice with extensive experience in CE marking, MDR/IVDR compliance, and market access for MedDevs and IVDs.
team.expert2_bio: Expert in clinical trials and pharmaceutical regulation. Brings extensive academic and industry experience in clinical investigation management, regulatory submissions, and evidence generation for medical devices and IVDs. Specializes in clinical performance studies under MDR/IVDR.
team.expert3_bio: Expert in health technology assessment (HTA) and medical devices. Specializes in value-based analysis, cost-effectiveness evaluation, and market access strategies for medical devices and IVDs across European markets. Deep knowledge of ОЗТ (Оценка на здравни технологии).
team.expert4_bio: Specialist in medical device regulation and compliance. Provides hands-on support for technical dossier preparation, conformity assessment processes, quality management systems, and regulatory documentation for CE marking under MDR/IVDR.
team.expert5_bio: Pharmaceutical specialist with expertise in medical devices and IVDs. Brings a unique cross-disciplinary perspective bridging pharmaceutical regulation and medical device compliance. Specializes in clinical evaluation, pharmacovigilance integration, and post-market surveillance.
team.cta_p: Get in touch to discuss how our team can support your medical device, IVD, or Medtech project.

services.hero_p: Comprehensive consulting for medical devices, IVDs, HTA, CE marking, clinical trials, and regulatory compliance across Europe.
services.s1_p1: Our team provides comprehensive support for medical device and IVD manufacturers navigating the complex EU regulatory landscape under MDR (EU 2017/745) and IVDR (EU 2017/746).
services.s1_p2: We help companies from initial concept through market launch and beyond, ensuring full compliance with European regulations while optimizing time-to-market.
services.s1_li2: Classification of medical devices and IVDs
services.s2_p1: Expert guidance on clinical performance studies, clinical evaluations, and evidence generation required for CE marking of medical devices and IVDs.
services.s2_p2: We support manufacturers in planning, conducting, and reporting clinical investigations in compliance with EU MDR/IVDR requirements.
services.s2_li2: Clinical investigation planning and protocol design
services.s2_box_li1: Gap analysis of existing clinical data
services.s3_p1: We guide manufacturers through the entire conformity assessment process, from selecting the appropriate Notified Body to achieving CE marking certification.
services.s3_p2: Our expertise ensures a smooth process, minimizing delays and maximizing the likelihood of successful first-time approval.
services.s3_li3: Technical file preparation for NB review
services.s3_li4: Response to NB queries and deficiencies
services.s4_p1: After obtaining your CE marking, ongoing regulatory obligations continue. We provide comprehensive post-market support to ensure continued compliance and market success.
services.s4_li6: Market access strategy for EU member states
services.s5_p1: End-to-end clinical trial management for medical devices and IVDs, ensuring regulatory compliance and high-quality evidence generation.
services.s5_p2: From protocol design through regulatory submission, our experienced team manages every aspect of your clinical program.
services.s6_p1: Expert support for secondary manufacturing processes including packaging, labelling, sterilization, and distribution of medical devices and IVDs.
services.s6_p2: We help manufacturers optimize their secondary manufacturing operations while maintaining full regulatory compliance.

clinical.hero_p: End-to-end clinical investigation management for medical devices and IVDs under EU MDR/IVDR.
clinical.overview_p1: Clinical trials for medical devices are essential for demonstrating safety and performance, and are a key requirement under the EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR 2017/745) and In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (IVDR 2017/746).
clinical.overview_p2: Our team of clinical research professionals manages the entire clinical trial lifecycle, from feasibility assessment and protocol design through to the final clinical study report and regulatory submission.
clinical.overview_p3: We work with medical device manufacturers, IVD companies, and MedTech innovators across Europe to generate robust clinical evidence supporting CE marking and market access.
clinical.box1_title: Why Choose CEHP for Clinical Trials?
clinical.box1_li1: Deep expertise in MDR/IVDR clinical requirements
clinical.box1_li5: Efficient study timelines and cost management
clinical.execution_li2: Site initiation, monitoring, and close-out visits
clinical.cta_title: Ready to Start Your Clinical Program?
clinical.cta_p: Contact our team to discuss your clinical trial needs and how we can support your path to CE marking.

manufacturing.hero_p: Comprehensive manufacturing, packaging, labelling, and distribution support for medical devices and IVDs.
manufacturing.overview_p1: Secondary manufacturing of medical devices and in vitro diagnostic medical devices (IVMD) encompasses all processes that take place after primary manufacturing, including packaging, labelling, sterilization, and preparation for distribution.
manufacturing.overview_p2: CEHP provides expert consulting and operational support to ensure your secondary manufacturing processes meet all regulatory requirements under MDR (EU 2017/745) and IVDR (EU 2017/746), while optimizing efficiency and quality.
manufacturing.overview_p3: Whether you are establishing new manufacturing operations or optimizing existing ones, our team brings the expertise needed to navigate the complex regulatory landscape of medical device manufacturing in Europe.
manufacturing.cta_p: Contact us to learn how CEHP can support your secondary manufacturing needs for medical devices and IVDs.

qa.hero_p: Find answers to common questions about medical device regulation, CE marking, clinical trials, and HTA.
qa.section_subtitle: Expert answers to the most common questions about medical device regulation and our services.
qa.q1: What is the EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR)?
qa.a1: The EU MDR (Regulation 2017/745) is the regulatory framework governing medical devices in the European Union. It replaced the Medical Devices Directive (MDD) and sets stricter requirements for clinical evidence, post-market surveillance, and traceability of medical devices. The MDR applies to all medical devices (MedDevs) placed on the EU market and requires manufacturers to demonstrate conformity through a comprehensive technical dossier and, for higher-risk devices, involvement of a Notified Body for CE marking.
qa.q2: What is the difference between MDR and IVDR?
qa.a2: The MDR (EU 2017/745) governs medical devices, while the IVDR (EU 2017/746) specifically regulates in vitro diagnostic medical devices (IVDs). While both regulations share similar principles regarding safety, performance, and post-market obligations, the IVDR has specific requirements for performance evaluation, including analytical and clinical performance studies unique to IVDs. Both require CE marking for market access in Europe.
qa.q3: What is CE marking and why is it required?
qa.a3: CE marking is the mandatory conformity marking for medical devices sold in the European Economic Area (EEA). It indicates that a device meets all applicable requirements of the MDR or IVDR and has undergone the appropriate conformity assessment procedure. Without CE marking, a medical device or IVD cannot be legally placed on the European market. The process involves preparing a technical dossier, conducting clinical evaluation, and for most devices, obtaining certification from a Notified Body.
qa.a4: Health Technology Assessment (HTA), known in Bulgarian as Оценка на здравни технологии (ОЗТ), is a systematic evaluation of the properties, effects, and impacts of health technologies including medical devices. HTA assesses clinical effectiveness, cost-effectiveness, safety, and broader societal implications to support informed healthcare decision-making. For MedTech companies, HTA is increasingly important for market access and reimbursement decisions across EU member states.
qa.q5: What are clinical trials for medical devices?
qa.a5: Clinical trials (clinical investigations) for medical devices are systematic studies designed to assess the safety and performance of a device in human subjects. Under the MDR, clinical investigations must follow strict protocols compliant with ISO 14155 and require approval from national competent authorities and ethics committees. They are essential for generating the clinical evidence required for CE marking, particularly for higher-risk and novel medical devices.
qa.q6: What is a technical dossier for medical devices?
qa.a6: A technical dossier (technical documentation) is a comprehensive collection of documentation that demonstrates a medical device meets all applicable regulatory requirements under the MDR or IVDR. It typically includes: device description and specifications, design and manufacturing information, risk management file (ISO 14971), clinical evaluation report, biocompatibility assessment, labelling and instructions for use, and post-market surveillance plan. Technical dossier preparation is a core service offered by CEHP.
qa.q7: What is secondary manufacturing of medical devices?
qa.a7: Secondary manufacturing refers to processes performed on medical devices after primary production, including packaging, labelling, sterilization, kitting, and preparation for distribution. It is subject to strict regulatory requirements under the MDR/IVDR, including GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) and GDP (Good Distribution Practice) compliance. CEHP supports manufacturers in establishing and optimizing secondary manufacturing operations while maintaining full regulatory compliance.
qa.q8: How can CEHP help with market access in Europe?
qa.a8: CEHP provides comprehensive market access support for medical device and IVD manufacturers entering or expanding within the European market. Our services include regulatory strategy development, CE marking support, HTA dossier preparation, reimbursement strategy, pricing analysis, distribution channel setup, and ongoing regulatory compliance management. We combine deep regulatory expertise with practical market knowledge to help MedTech companies successfully launch and commercialize their products across EU member states.
qa.a9: Post-market surveillance (PMS) is a systematic process for collecting and analyzing data about the performance and safety of a medical device once it is on the market. Under the MDR/IVDR, manufacturers are required to proactively plan and implement PMS systems, produce PMS reports and Periodic Safety Update Reports (PSURs), and conduct Post-Market Clinical Follow-up (PMCF) studies. PMS is essential for maintaining CE marking and ensuring continued patient safety.
qa.q10: What is the role of a Notified Body?
qa.a10: A Notified Body is an organization designated by an EU member state to assess the conformity of medical devices before they can be placed on the market. For higher-risk medical devices (Class IIa, IIb, III) and certain IVDs, Notified Body involvement is mandatory for the conformity assessment process. The Notified Body reviews the technical documentation, conducts audits of the quality management system, and issues CE certificates. CEHP assists manufacturers throughout the entire Notified Body engagement process.
qa.cta_p: Our team is ready to answer your specific questions about medical device regulation, clinical trials, HTA, and more.
